issues_found: Source is a bank supervisory examination manual describing internal examiner procedures for capital assessment - fundamentally mismatched with TARGET of 'macro catalysts'
Source contains no market-moving content: no rate decisions, data releases, speeches, monetary policy statements, or trading-relevant commentary
Document type is procedural/supervisory guidance rather than a macro catalyst or market structure source
Published_at shows May 2021 but URL filename (202602) suggests a February 2026 version - date inconsistency should be verified
The generated record already correctly identifies this as not relevant, yet still has status 'review_queue' - self-contradictory
human_review_reason: The record already contains accurate self-assessment indicating the source is not relevant to macro catalysts. No human review needed - auto-reject is appropriate.

corrected_fields.summary: This is Section 3000 of the Federal Reserve's Commercial Bank Examination Manual covering capital adequacy assessment procedures for bank examiners, effective November 2020. The document describes Regulation Q requirements, components of regulatory capital (CET1, Additional Tier 1, Tier 2), and examination procedures for assessing bank financial condition. This is an internal supervisory manual, not a source that discusses macro catalysts, market-moving events, or trading implications.
corrected_fields.why_it_matters: This document is a supervisory examination manual for Federal Reserve examiners assessing bank capital adequacy. It is not relevant to macro catalysts or market structure in the trading sense. It has no market-moving content and is intended for bank regulatory supervisors, not market participants. Recommend rejection from macro catalysts archive.
